texto: There is a lot of work ahead of us, as we endeavor to rescue the planet from ourselves, and we are likely to be at this work for a very long time. Perhaps we could come at it from the wisdom of the non-self perspective, rather than the passions of the "world is mine" point of view.

texto: By identifying impermanence as a fundamental characteristic of existence itself, rather than a problem to be solved, the Buddhists are encouraging us to let go our hold on illusory solidity and learn to swim freely in the sea of change.
explicacao: Citação de Andrew Olendzki sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “By identifying impermanence as a fundamental characteristic of existence itself, rather than a pr…” articula reflexao, buddhism, impermanence em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e impermanence.

frase_original: By identifying impermanence as a fundamental characteristic of existence itself, rather than a problem to be solved, the Buddhists are encouraging us to let go our hold on illusory solidity and learn to swim freely in the sea of change.

texto: Even if we were very good at making everything outside of ourselves be just the way we ourselves want it to be (a ludicrous thought, you must admit), we could fundamentally never get everything perfect: because our desires are always changing, because they are often conflicting, and because the changes of the environment can never keep up with the pace of the wanting mind. The satisfaction of desire as a strategy for happiness will always be a doomed enterprise.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Andrew Olendzki sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “Even if we were very good at making everything outside of ourselves be just the way we ourselves …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a felicidade, buddhism, clinging, útil em momentos de mudanca e clinging.

texto: The mess we are making of our planet is caused by our own greed, hatred, and delusion. Aside from the existential afflictions of aging, death, and at least some of the illnesses, every instance we see of human misery, injustice, affliction. or sufficient and pain will, upon sufficient and sometimes even cursory investigation, be shown to be rooted in the attachment, aversion, or ignorance of some person or some group of people together.

texto: Mindfulness means being present to whatever is happening here and now - when mindfulness is strong, there is no room left in the mind for wanting something else. With less liking and disliking of what arises, there is less pushing and pulling on the world, less defining of the threshold between self and other, resulting in a reduced construction of self. As the influence of self diminishes, suffering diminishes in proportion.
explicacao: Citação de Andrew Olendzki sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Mindfulness means being present to whatever is happening here and now - when mindfulness is stron…” articula reflexao, buddhism, mindfulness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como mindfulness e no-self.
